audit_text: Blocking followups don't recurse (auto_driver.py:501-507). Child item is created but driver continues parent immediately. Spec: pause parent, drive child to terminal, resume parent.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/checklists.md §Sentinels — 'Followup, blocking. A new child item is appended under the current item. The driver recurses into the child before completing the parent.'
verdict_evidence: src/bearings/agent/auto_driver.py:499-507 — _apply_followups inserts a child item via checklists_db.create(parent_item_id=item.id) and returns; the surrounding _run_leg_turns loop then continues the parent leg. _drive_item never pauses the current item to drive the new child to terminal. No call into a recursive _drive_item(child_item) before the outer loop's next-pending-item pick (lines 217-228) — and that pick would only see the child if the parent has already terminated. Spec at docs/behavior/checklists.md:71 mandates recurse-then-resume.
acceptance_criteria: When a leg emits SENTINEL_KIND_FOLLOWUP_BLOCKING with a label, the driver creates the child item under the current item AND drives that child to a terminal per-item state (item_done, item_blocked, item_failed, or skip) before resuming any further leg turns on the parent.
Recursion respects DriverConfig.max_followup_depth — beyond the cap, the followup is treated as malformed and ignored per docs/behavior/checklists.md:81.

An integration test in tests/test_auto_driver_integration.py asserts: parent leg emits a blocking followup; the next driver action is to drive the child item; the parent leg does not resume until the child's terminal sentinel is recorded.
Counters reflect both items: child item's terminal state ticks the appropriate counter (items_completed / items_blocked / items_failed / items_skipped) before the parent's terminal counter ticks.
executor_brief: auto_driver._apply_followups creates blocking-followup children but never pauses the parent leg to drive the child. Per docs/behavior/checklists.md:71 a blocking followup must pause the parent, drive the child to terminal, then resume. Refactor _drive_item / _apply_followups so blocking followups invoke a recursive _drive_item(child) before returning to the parent's leg loop, honoring max_followup_depth as the recursion guard.

audit_text: Checklist auto-close cascade missing (routes/checklists.py:278-287). Marking item checked does not (a) close its paired chat, (b) walk parent chain to check all siblings done, (c) close checklist session when all root items complete.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/checklists.md §Item ↔ chat-session linking (paired chats) — 'Checking a paired item closes the chat. ... Closing cascades up: each parent whose children are all complete auto-closes, and the parent checklist session auto-closes when every root item is complete.'
verdict_evidence: src/bearings/web/routes/checklists.py:278-287 (`check_item`) calls only `checklists_db.mark_checked(db, item_id)` and returns. db/checklists.py:396-419 (`mark_checked`) issues a single UPDATE that sets `checked_at` and clears `blocked_*`; it does NOT (a) close the paired chat session via sessions_db.close, (b) walk the item's parent chain to derive AND-of-children completion, or (c) close the checklist session when every root item has `checked_at IS NOT NULL`. No cascade hook anywhere else in the call chain.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/checklist-items/{id}/check on a paired leaf closes the paired chat session (sessions.closed_at = now) and broadcasts the close via SessionsBroadcaster.publish_upsert.
When the checked item's siblings (under the same parent_item_id) all have checked_at IS NOT NULL, the parent item's `checked_at` is also set; this walks recursively up the parent chain.

When all root items (parent_item_id IS NULL) of the checklist have checked_at IS NOT NULL, the checklist session itself (sessions row with kind='checklist') is closed via sessions_db.close and broadcast.
Auto-close is one-directional per docs/behavior/checklists.md:35 — POST /uncheck on a previously-checked item does NOT reopen any closed parent / chat / checklist.
Tests assert each cascade branch: paired-chat closes, parent fills when last sibling checks, checklist closes when last root checks.
executor_brief: The check_item route just sets checked_at; the spec requires a cascade. Implement: (1) close the leaf's paired chat and broadcast; (2) walk parent chain — when every child of a parent is checked, set the parent's checked_at; (3) when every root item is checked, close the checklist session and broadcast. Keep auto-close one-directional (uncheck does not reopen). Cascade can live in a new db/checklists helper called from the route handler.

audit_text: Chat deletion does not clear item pointer. Deleting a paired chat leaves chat_session_id pointing to a dead row; item renders broken link.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/paired-chats.md §Behavior under one-side-closed — 'Chat deleted: The pair pointer is cleared from the item side; the leaf reverts to unpaired and shows the 💬 Work on this button as if no chat had ever existed.'
verdict_evidence: schema.sql:307 declares `FOREIGN KEY (chat_session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL`. db/connection.py:79 enables `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON` on every load. db/sessions.py:1020-1039 (`delete`) uses the bootstrapped long-lived connection so the FK fires; its docstring explicitly cites the contract from docs/behavior/paired-chats.md. The schema FK has been in place since item 0.4 (commit 0e92965d, 2026-04-28), pre-dating the 2026-05-07 audit — the audit appears to have missed the FK. The bug as described is not reachable in HEAD.
executor_brief: No action — bug not present in HEAD. The ON DELETE SET NULL FK on checklist_items.chat_session_id plus PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON ensures pair pointer clears on session row deletion. If extra confidence is needed, an integration test could assert the FK fires (insert item with chat_session_id, delete the session, assert the item's chat_session_id is NULL).

audit_text: Spawn-from-reply UI never wired. Backend route (routes/spawn_from_reply.py) complete, but `+ SPAWN` action pill does not exist anywhere in frontend conversation pane. Zero frontend references. Distinct defect class — backend-ahead-of-frontend.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/paired-chats.md §Spawn from reply (gap-cycle-03-007) — 'Every completed assistant message in a non-paired chat session displays a ＋ SPAWN action pill ... Clicking it: Calls POST /api/sessions/{parent_id}/spawn_from_reply/{message_id}'
verdict_evidence: MessageTurn.svelte:43 imports `spawnFromReply`; :163-171 `handleSpawn` invokes the API call and navigates to the new session; :474-485 renders the `＋ SPAWN` button gated on `!isPaired` (paired-chat suppression per spec line 94). Frontend test exists at MessageTurn.test.ts. Resolving commit `de9f0137` (2026-05-07 01:14:14) 'feat: spawn-from-reply endpoint + SPAWN pill (gap-cycle-03-007)' lands the wiring; subsequent commit `c612a639` is a follow-up.
executor_brief: No action — bug fixed in commit de9f0137 (gap-cycle-03-007). MessageTurn.svelte renders the ＋ SPAWN pill on completed assistant turns when the session is not paired.

audit_text: visit_existing + closed-chat unchecked (auto_driver.py:285-291). Driver checks chat_session_id is set but not that the session is still open. Closed paired chat = hard failure instead of skip.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/checklists.md §Run-control surface — 'Visit-existing-sessions toggle. When enabled before Start, the driver reuses each item's already-paired chat (if any) instead of spawning a fresh one. Items with no paired chat (or a closed paired chat) are skipped and contribute to the skipped count.'
verdict_evidence: src/bearings/agent/auto_driver.py:285-291 — `if self._config.visit_existing and leg_number == 1 and item.chat_session_id: leg_session_id = item.chat_session_id`. Only the pointer existence is checked. There is no `await sessions_db.is_closed(...)` (or equivalent) gate; `_LOG.debug` then falls through to the runner factory which (per behavior contract) raises if the session is closed/gone. No code branch turns a closed paired chat into a `skip` outcome with `items_skipped += 1` per spec.
acceptance_criteria: When `visit_existing=True` and `item.chat_session_id` is set, the driver checks the session's open state (e.g. via `sessions_db.is_closed`) before reusing it.

If the paired chat is closed, the item is recorded as `skipped` (using `_record_skip` or the equivalent path), `items_skipped` ticks, the driver advances to the next pending item, and no leg is spawned for it.
If the paired chat is open, behavior is unchanged from today.
An integration test asserts: visit_existing run with a closed paired chat yields `items_skipped == 1` and zero `legs_spawned` for that item.
executor_brief: auto_driver:285-291 reuses item.chat_session_id without checking whether the session is closed. Per docs/behavior/checklists.md:60 a closed paired chat under visit_existing must skip the item (and tick items_skipped). Add an open-state check (sessions_db.is_closed) before reuse; on closed, route through _record_skip and `continue` the outer loop.

audit_text: Followup label not validated; empty/whitespace labels create invalid items.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/checklists.md §Item edit / add / delete / reorder — 'Add. Typing a label in the Add-item input and pressing Enter creates a new item ... An empty label is rejected at the boundary.'
verdict_evidence: ChecklistItem.__post_init__ at db/checklists.py:91-100 only checks `if not self.label:` (truthy). Whitespace-only `'   '` is truthy in Python and slips through; an item with a blank-looking label lands in the DB. auto_driver._apply_followups (lines 499-514) only checks `and label` truthy before calling `checklists_db.create`, so a sentinel-emitted whitespace label is also accepted.
acceptance_criteria: ChecklistItem.__post_init__ rejects labels whose `.strip()` is empty (raises ValueError 'must be non-empty after stripping whitespace').
checklists_db.create / update_label normalize the label by stripping leading/trailing whitespace before insert/update (or reject — pick one per coding standards and apply consistently).
auto_driver._apply_followups skips followup findings whose label fails the same check rather than letting db/__post_init__ raise.
A db-layer test asserts that `'   '` (whitespace-only) raises ValueError; a route-layer test asserts POST /api/checklists/{id}/items returns 422 for whitespace-only labels.
executor_brief: Empty-string is rejected; whitespace-only labels slip through `if not self.label:`. Tighten ChecklistItem.__post_init__ to test `if not self.label.strip():` (and apply the same gate at update_label / followup creation paths). Match the existing `non-empty after stripping whitespace` discipline already used by message bodies (db/messages.py:118).

audit_text: No recovery if leg session vanishes mid-run; driver halts cryptically.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/checklists.md §When the user starts / pauses / stops a run — 'If the server restarts mid-run, the run is rehydrated on next boot — the user sees the status line resume against the same item rather than the run vanishing.' (failure-recovery context)
verdict_evidence: auto_driver.drive (lines 181-202) only catches `StopRequested`; any other exception propagates out of the asyncio task. _run_leg_turns (lines 406-461) calls `runtime.run_turn` without try/except. auto_driver_runtime.run_turn (lines 112-126) calls `runner_factory(leg_session_id)` which raises if the session row is gone — that exception bubbles uncaught. The registry's done-callback (`web/routes/checklists.py:547`) only unregisters the driver; it does not finalize the run row to a coherent terminal state, so a vanished-session crash leaves the run row in `running` with no status line update.
acceptance_criteria: When `runtime.run_turn` or `runtime.spawn_leg` raises a session-vanished error (the runtime SHOULD signal this with a typed exception — e.g. `LegSessionVanished` — to distinguish from generic errors), the driver records the current item as `failed` with reason 'leg_session_vanished' and either halts (under halt policy) or advances (under skip policy) per the existing failure-policy plumbing.
drive() catches the typed exception and finalizes the run row to a `finished` state with a coherent outcome ('Halted: leg session vanished') — never leaves the run in `running` with no status update.
The route layer's done-callback handles task exceptions explicitly (logs + finalizes the run row) so the user sees a clear status-line message instead of a silent halt.
An integration test simulates a leg-session-vanish (delete session row mid-turn) and asserts the run row reaches `finished` state with the expected outcome string.
executor_brief: auto_driver.drive only catches StopRequested; any other exception (e.g. session row deleted out from under the leg) propagates out of the asyncio task and leaves the run row stuck in `running`. Add a typed `LegSessionVanished` exception raised by the runtime when its runner factory cannot find the session, catch it in drive(), record an item failure with a clear reason, and finalize the run state. Update the done-callback to log + finalize on unhandled task exceptions.

audit_text: Sidebar does not push leg-cutover events; users do not see fast-cycling rows without manual refresh.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/checklists.md §Sentinels — 'the driver kills the current paired chat's runner, spawns a successor leg ... The user sees the chat row respawn — the previous chat closes, a fresh one opens against the same item.' / §Sidebar visibility of paired-chat activity — 'fast-cycling sequence of new chat rows for the same item'
verdict_evidence: auto_driver._drive_item:316 calls `runtime.teardown_leg(leg_session_id=...)`. auto_driver_runtime.teardown_leg (lines 128-136) only does `self._pressure_by_leg.pop(leg_session_id, None)` and explicitly notes 'runner stays alive'. No sessions_db.close call, no SessionsBroadcaster.publish_upsert with closed_at on the predecessor leg. _leg_session_factory (web/app.py:197) DOES broadcast NEW leg sessions, but the previous leg's chat row never gets a close event — it accumulates as an open chat. Spec line 69 explicitly says 'the previous chat closes', and line 121 promises a 'fast-cycling sequence' that requires close events.
acceptance_criteria: On handoff (and on any per-item terminal that ends a leg), the predecessor leg's chat session is closed via sessions_db.close and broadcast via SessionsBroadcaster.publish_upsert (so the sidebar shows the row move to its Closed group).
The successor leg's spawn continues to broadcast its upsert (already covered by _leg_session_factory:197).
The teardown_leg contract is updated so DriverRuntime callers can plumb the SessionsBroadcaster (or equivalent close-and-broadcast hook) in from the web/ layer without auto_driver.py importing web/.

An integration test exercises a leg cutover (handoff sentinel) and asserts the predecessor session is closed and a `publish_upsert` was emitted with `closed_at != null` for it before the successor leg's upsert.
executor_brief: teardown_leg only drops a pressure-cache entry; it leaves the predecessor chat session open, so the spec's 'previous chat closes' / 'fast-cycling rows' behavior never materialises. Plumb a close-and-broadcast hook into the runtime (e.g. add `close_session_callback: Callable[[str], Awaitable[None]]` to AutoDriverRuntimeOptions; the web/ layer wires it to sessions_db.close + sessions_broadcaster.publish_upsert). Call it from teardown_leg on handoff/terminal cutovers.

audit_text: TODO.md has two dead entries claiming PairedChatIndicator is unmounted and ChecklistChat was deleted — both are in place.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/paired-chats.md §From the chat side (PairedChatIndicator breadcrumb) + docs/behavior/checklists.md §ChecklistChat
verdict_evidence: TODO.md:202-218 still asserts 'PairedChatIndicator wiring (deferred)' / 'unmounted because no backend endpoint exposes the (parent_title, item_label) lookup'. ConversationHeader.svelte:65 imports the component and :307 mounts `<PairedChatIndicator …/>`. TODO.md:258-266 still asserts 'ChecklistChat — deleted (no documented role) … Removed in commit (this sweep)'. The component file exists at frontend/src/lib/components/checklist/ChecklistChat.svelte and ChecklistView.svelte:90 imports it / :332 renders `<ChecklistChat {checklistId} />`. docs/behavior/checklists.md §ChecklistChat (lines 124-137) now documents the panel.
acceptance_criteria: TODO.md no longer claims PairedChatIndicator is unmounted (the entry at lines 202-218 is removed or updated to a strikethrough with the resolving commit hash per CLAUDE.md TODO discipline).
TODO.md no longer claims ChecklistChat was deleted (the entry at lines 258-266 is removed or updated similarly).

The strike-out trailer cites the commit that mounted PairedChatIndicator and the commit that re-introduced (or never removed) ChecklistChat, per CLAUDE.md 'strike it from TODO.md in the same commit that resolves it and cite the resolving commit hash in the entry's removal trailer'.
executor_brief: TODO.md still carries two dead deferral entries — PairedChatIndicator is mounted in ConversationHeader and ChecklistChat is mounted in ChecklistView. Strike both entries per CLAUDE.md TODO discipline (`~^^^~ removed line ~^^^~ — resolved by <commit>`-style trailer) so the file accurately reflects current state.
